LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. AS TO PLAINTIFF ROXANA MORALES' PETITION FOR ORDER RELIEVING ROXANA MORALES AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM FOR J.W., Z. W., AND ZA. W FROM GOVT CODE SECTION 945.4, THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT PREVIOUSLY CONCLUDED THAT BECAUSE A "(1) PLAINTIFF'S LATE-CLAIM APPLICATION WAS FILED WITHIN ONE YEAR OF THE ACCRUAL OF HIS CAUSE OF ACTION, (2) PLAINTIFF WAS A MINOR FOR THE ENTIRE ONE HUNDRED-DAY CLAIM PERIOD, AND (3) ANY DELAY IN FILING THE LATE-CLAIM APPLICATION WAS CLEARLY NOT ATTRIBUTABLE TO ANY LACK OF DILIGENCE ON THE PART OF THE MINOR HIMSELF, THE COUNTY WAS UNDER AN OBLIGATION TO ACCEPT THE LATE CLAIM UNDER SECTION 911.6 . . . ." (SEE HERNANDEZ V. COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (1986) 42 CAL.3D 1020, 1031.) RESPONDENTS UNPERSUASIVELY ASK THE SUPERIOR COURT TO CONTRAVENE A HOLDING OF THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Off Calendar Mar-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. DAVID FUJIMOTO, ESQ. and SANDRA L. RIBERA, ESQ. of Ribera Law Firm APC, Tel No. 415-576-1600 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. MARINA B. PITTS, ESQ. of Leone & Alberts, Tel No. 925-974-8600 appearing for Defendants San Francisco Unified School District, Jennifer Steiner, Larry Alegre, and Brenda Arias;
Depity City Attorney NEWTON OLDFATHER, ESQ. of San Francisco Office of the City Attorney, Tel No. 415-554-3800 appearing for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

The Court's tentative ruling required parties to appear at hearing. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court rules as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Defendant City and County of San Francisco shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature which may be e-mailed to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 (302/HK)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition For Order Relieving Roxana Morales As Guardian Ad Litem For J W Z W And Za W From Govt Code Section 945.4
Off Calendar Oct-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF ROXANA MORALES' Petition For Order Relieving Roxana Morales As Guardian Ad Litem For J.W., Z. W., and Za. W From Govt Code Section 945.4, the California Supreme Court previously concluded that because a "(1) plaintiff's late-claim application was filed within one year of the accrual of his cause of action, (2) plaintiff was a minor for the entire one hundred-day claim period, and (3) any delay in filing the late-claim application was clearly not attributable to any lack of diligence on the part of the minor himself, the county was under an obligation to accept the late claim under section 911.6 . . . ." (See Hernandez v. County of Los Angeles (1986) 42 Cal.3d 1020, 1031.) Respondents unpersuasively ask the Superior Court to contravene a holding of the California Supreme Court. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

payments.description: REQ OR STIP AND ORDER W/O HEARING
